The open-fronted barn is currently being used extensively as a feeding perch by Barn Owls (Tyto alba) as evidenced by the presence of owl pellets throughout the barn. If young are present, care must be taken to avoid any disturbance, as Barn Owls are a Schedule 1 species under the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981.

The barn is also being used routinely and extensively as a night feeding roost by bats, most likely Brown Long-eared Bats (Plecotus auritus) as well as potentially other species, as evidenced by extensive feeding remains throughout the barn. As such, this site is protected under a range of legislation.
The barn is being used as a nest site by House Sparrows (Passer domesticus).

CCTV inspection of the network was carried out on 4 March 2026.  This inspection confirmed the presence of an overflow pipeline located within the sea wall next to the railway line, known as the Black Bridge Outfall (grid reference SX 96771 77070). This outfall is designed to operate in specific circumstances. It acts as a secondary outfall for Sandy Lane Pumping Station and Dawlish Wastewater Treatment Works during certain storm and tidal conditions, and it also serves as the main outfall for three storm overflows and for Sea Lawns Pumping Station.

For discharges to coastal waters, we normally expect that the soffit of the outfall should be located below the mean low water of spring tides (MLWS).  The Black Bridge Outfall is currently above MLWS however, SWW have stated they will remedy this as soon as practicable. We are investigating this issue in line with our enforcement and prosecution policy.

undertaken during the period October to February of any one year, i.e. outside of the documented breeding~
season.~
Where works are to be performed in close vicinity to the badger sett location, appropriate mitigation should be~
employed in that staff are briefed, no vehicles are to be driven into the field within 30 metres of the badger sett~
location and ground spikes are not to be installed within 20 metres of any sett entrance and are to be removed~
at the end of the survey day. This accords with previously published Best Practice to avoid potential for~
disturbance to badgers.
